definitions.text: Physically elevated, extending above a base or average level: Very elevated; extending or being far above a base; tall; lofty.
examples: The balloon rose high in the sky. The wall was high. a high mountain
the pitch (or: the ball) was high

definitions.text: Very elevated; extending or being far above a base; tall; lofty.
examples: The balloon rose high in the sky. The wall was high. a high mountain

definitions.text: Relatively elevated; rising or raised above the average or normal level from which elevation is measured.
In or to an elevated position.
examples: How high above land did you fly?
The desks were piled high with magazines.

definitions.text: In or at a great value.
examples: Costs have grown higher this year again.

definitions.text: At a pitch of great frequency.
examples: I certainly can't sing that high.

definitions.text: A high point or position, literally (as, an elevated place; a superior region; a height; the sky; heaven) or figuratively (as, a point of success or achievement; a time when things are at their best, greatest, most numerous, maximum, etc).
examples: It was one of the highs of his career.

definitions.text: The maximum atmospheric temperature recorded at a particular location, especially during one 24-hour period.
A period of euphoria, from excitement or from an intake of drugs.
examples: That pill gave me a high for a few hours, before I had a comedown.
